claims.title: Executive summary keeps measurable lanes on their native bases
claims.statement: The opening section preserves lane separation instead of collapsing obligations, outlays, cumulative military lines, and domestic program examples into one number.
claims.confidence: See linked section and source confidence records.

claims.title: Methodology distinguishes legal status categories and number types
claims.statement: Qualified status categories, waiting-period rules, appropriations, obligations, outlays, and transfer values are treated as distinct analytical objects.
claims.confidence: See linked section and source confidence records.

claims.title: International assistance obligations and disbursements are not interchangeable
claims.statement: Section 3 preserves separate accounting states while carrying the unresolved beneficiary-capture share as an open question.
claims.confidence: See linked section and source confidence records.

claims.title: Ukraine and Israel examples are illustrative and non-additive
claims.statement: Section 4 exists to show stage differences and beneficiary chains without adding its examples back into the running totals.

claims.title: Ukraine stage labels cannot be netted casually
claims.statement: Appropriations, drawdowns, disbursements, and delivered-value estimates sit at different stages and remain analytically separate.

claims.title: Military aid keeps net-new lanes separate from already-counted foreign assistance

claims.title: ORR is owned as a domestic lane, not an overseas add-on
claims.statement: Refugee and entrant assistance stays in one canonical domestic section so it is not double-counted against foreign-assistance accounts.
claims.confidence: See linked section and source confidence records.

claims.title: ORR provider capture remains a first-class unresolved limitation
claims.statement: The public record identifies the ORR lane but does not yet publish a defensible entrant-versus-provider split.

claims.title: Emergency Medicaid is measurable on a federal-plus-state basis, not a federal-only basis

claims.title: SNAP noncitizen dollars are not backfilled with modeled estimates

claims.title: SSI is one of the clearest direct-cash domestic lanes
